body:
## Summary
- Move the citizenship requirements route from static light `colors.*` reads to active theme colors from the shared theme context.
- Keep Search provenance source notes on dark-theme surface/border tokens so expanded source notes render correctly in dark mode.
- Wire Settings to the shared theme context and scoped accessibility persistence warning while updating the scroll-parity guard for the new import shape.

## Validation
- `NODE_OPTIONS="--v8-pool-size=1" node --test scripts/theme-discipline.test.js tests/content-search-route-accessibility-parity.test.js tests/content-citizenship-requirements-parity.test.js tests/content-settings-route-scroll-parity.test.js --test-name-pattern "theme|dark|search|citizenship|requirements|colorsForThemeMode|radius|checkbox|source|settings route keeps mobile content"`
- `npm run typecheck -- --pretty false`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 EXPO_NO_TELEMETRY=1 NODE_OPTIONS="--v8-pool-size=1" npm run build:web:export -- --max-workers 2`
- `CI=1 EXPO_NO_TELEMETRY=1 NODE_OPTIONS="--v8-pool-size=1" npm run test:e2e -- tests/e2e/theme-mode-utility-routes.spec.ts tests/e2e/search-accessibility.spec.ts --workers=1`
- `npm run validate:content`

## Known Current-Main Guard
The task's broader V1 command still fails one pre-existing assertion: `tests/v1-1-accessibility.test.js` expects `app/(tabs)/practice.tsx` to consume `useThemeColors()`. That belongs to the already claimed `NATIVE-DARK-THEME-APP-WIDE-TOKENS-1` scope and is not changed here.
title: Theme utility routes use active color tokens
